perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will commence on the Signature D ate and shall remain binding and in force for a period of five years thereafter or such longer period as the Parties may agree to in writing ("Term ").
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will commence on the Signature Date and shall remain binding and in force indefinitely unless terminated by either party with 30 days' written notice ("Term ").
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the agreement lasts for five years or longer if agreed in writing. The modification states it lasts indefinitely but can be terminated with 30 days' notice. This creates an in-text contradiction, where the duration is both fixed and terminable, making it unclear how long the confidentiality obligations actually last and creating uncertainty about the "Term" of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will commence on the Signature D ate and shall remain binding and in force for a period of five years thereafter or such longer period as the Parties may agree to in writing ("Term ").
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement lasts for five years or longer if agreed in writing. The changed text states it lasts indefinitely but can be terminated with 30 days' notice. This creates an in-text contradiction, where the duration is both fixed and terminable. The contradiction is triggered with the Contradicted Text because the agreement cannot both have a fixed term and be indefinite with a 30-day termination clause. This follows the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition provided, as the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, " Confidential Information " shall include, without limitation, any information or other data relating to the Disclosing Party or any of its Affiliates, which is disclosed to the Receiving Party , whether prior to the conclusion of this Agreement, in terms of this Agreement or otherwise in connection with this Agreement or the Transaction (including but not limited to the Transaction itself and any negotiations in relation to the Transaction ), includ ing this Agreement, its contents and the negotiations leading up to the conclusion of this Agreement - 2.3.1 in whatever format whether written, oral or graphic, recorded or not (and if recorded, whether recorded in writing, on any electronic medium or 2 otherwis e howsoever) which is intended or by its nature could reasonably be expected to be confidential, which is disclosed or communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party , its Affiliates or any of their respective employees, representatives , office rs, directors and advis ers, or which otherwise comes to the knowledge of the Receiving Party, by whatever means, before or after the date on which this Agreement is signed by the last Party signing it (" Signature Date"); and 2.3.2 which includes, without derog ating from the aforegoing, all financial information, marketing information, products, product information, drawings, plans and specificat ions, operational and risk methodologies, production and operating procedures, computer data, programs and source codes, pricing, pric e lists and purchasing policies, information relating to costs, sources of materials, business relationships, services, customers and customer list s (whether actual or potential); technical information, techniq ues, know -how, trade secrets, operating methods and procedures, electronic and manual systems; all intellectual property; contractual arrangements; training schemes and programs; information relating to the Disclosing Party 's strategic objectives and plann ing for both its existing and future business needs; the contractual business and financial arrangements between the Disclosing Party and other third Parties with whom they have business arrangements of whatever nature; all information specifically relate d to the business of the Disclosing Party which is not readily available to a competitor of the Disclosing Party in the ordinary course of business; the existence and contents of any process related documentation and all information or data relating thereto disclosed to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party, its Affiliates or any of their respective employ ees, representatives, officers, directors and advis ers.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, " Confidential Information " shall include, without limitation, any information or other data relating to the Disclosing Party or any of its Affiliates, which is disclosed to the Receiving Party , whether prior to the conclusion of this Agreement, in terms of this Agreement or otherwise in connection with this Agreement or the Transaction (including but not limited to the Transaction itself and any negotiations in relation to the Transaction ), includ ing this Agreement, its contents and the negotiations leading up to the conclusion of this Agreement - 2.3.1 in whatever format whether written, oral or graphic, recorded or not (and if recorded, whether recorded in writing, on any electronic medium or 2 otherwis e howsoever) which is intended or by its nature could reasonably be expected to be confidential, which is disclosed or communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party , its Affiliates or any of their respective employees, representatives , office rs, directors and advis ers, or which otherwise comes to the knowledge of the Receiving Party, by whatever means, before or after the date on which this Agreement is signed by the last Party signing it ("Signature Date"); and 2.3.2 which includes all financial information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' by the Disclosing Party. Information not explicitly marked is not considered Confidential Information. This excludes all other categories of data originally included in the definition.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides a broad definition of "Confidential Information." The modification restricts it to only financial information explicitly marked as 'Confidential.' This creates an in-text contradiction, where the definition of Confidential Information is both broad and narrow, making it unclear which information is actually protected under the agreement. In particular it creates a direct contradiction with subsection 2.3.2 which indicates that it includes all financial information, marketing information, product related information and so forth. Now it only includes financial information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3.2 which includes, without derog ating from the aforegoing, all financial information, marketing information, products, product information, drawings, plans and specificat ions, operational and risk methodologies, production and operating procedures, computer data, programs and source codes, pricing, pric e lists and purchasing policies, information relating to costs, sources of materials, business relationships, services, customers and customer list s (whether actual or potential); technical information, techniq ues, know -how, trade secrets, operating methods and procedures, electronic and manual systems; all intellectual property; contractual arrangements; training schemes and programs; information relating to the Disclosing Party 's strategic objectives and plann ing for both its existing and future business needs; the contractual business and financial arrangements between the Disclosing Party and other third Parties with whom they have business arrangements of whatever nature; all information specifically relate d to the business of the Disclosing Party which is not readily available to a competitor of the Disclosing Party in the ordinary course of business; the existence and contents of any process related documentation and all information or data relating thereto disclosed to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party, its Affiliates or any of their respective employ ees, representatives, officers, directors and advis ers.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only include financial information explicitly marked as 'Confidential'. This directly contradicts the original definition in section 2.3.2, which broadly includes 'all financial information, marketing information, products, product information, drawings, plans and specificat ions, operational and risk methodologies, production and operating procedures, computer data, programs and source codes, pricing, pric e lists and purchasing policies, information relating to costs, sources of materials, business relationships, services, customers and customer list s (whether actual or potential); technical information, techniq ues, know -how, trade secrets, operating methods and procedures, electronic and manual systems; all intellectual property; contractual arrangements; training schemes and programs', among other things. The changed text's explicit exclusion of all other categories creates a clear and direct conflict and meets the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition because it creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2.4 not divulge or permit access to the Confiden tial Information to any person except to those of its employees , officers, directors , representatives , advisers and Affiliates who have a specific need to know such information in relation to the Transaction . Before revealing any Confidential Information to its employees , officers, directors , representatives , advisers and/or Affiliates , the Receiving Party shall be obliged to notify such employees , officers, directors, representatives , advisers and/or Affiliates of the confidential nature of t he disclosure and ensure that such employees , officers, directors, representatives , advisers and/or Affiliates are obliged not to disclose the Confidential Information to any unauthorised third party and not use the Confidential Information except as may b e strictly required in relation to the Transaction . The Receiving Party shall be liable for any damages incurred by the Disclosing Party as a result of any unauthorised disclosure of the Confidential Information made by the Receiving Party, it s employees, officers, directors, representatives and /or Affiliates; and
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2.4 not divulge or permit access to the Confiden tial Information to any person, including its employees, officers, directors, representatives, advisers and Affiliates. The Receiving Party shall not be liable for any damages incurred by the Disclosing Party as a result of any disclosure of the Confidential Information made by the Receiving Party, it s employees, officers, directors, representatives and /or Affiliates; and
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows disclosure to certain individuals with a need to know, while the modified text prohibits disclosure to *anyone*, including those same individuals. It also removes the liability on the Receiving Party. This creates an in-text contradiction within the clause itself. It first says you cannot disclose to anyone, but then removes the liability of doing so.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2.4 not divulge or permit access to the Confiden tial Information to any person except to those of its employees , officers, directors , representatives , advisers and Affiliates who have a specific need to know such information in relation to the Transaction . Before revealing any Confidential Information to its employees , officers, directors , representatives , advisers and/or Affiliates , the Receiving Party shall be obliged to notify such employees , officers, directors, representatives , advisers and/or Affiliates of the confidential nature of t he disclosure and ensure that such employees , officers, directors, representatives , advisers and/or Affiliates are obliged not to disclose the Confidential Information to any unauthorised third party and not use the Confidential Information except as may b e strictly required in relation to the Transaction . The Receiving Party shall be liable for any damages incurred by the Disclosing Party as a result of any unauthorised disclosure of the Confidential Information made by the Receiving Party, it s employees, officers, directors, representatives and /or Affiliates; and
perturbation.justification: The changed text in 4.2.4 initially prohibits divulging Confidential Information to *anyone*, including employees, officers, etc., directly contradicting the original text of 4.2.4 (which is also the contradicted text) that explicitly allows disclosure to specific individuals within the organization with a need to know. Furthermore, the changed text removes the liability for unauthorized disclosure, whereas the original text holds the Receiving Party liable for such disclosures. This creates a direct contradiction within the clause itself, where it first states no disclosure is allowed, and then removes the liability if the receiving party does disclose the information. This breaks compatibility with the original/contradicted section because it removes the explicit exception for specific individuals, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 The Disclosing Party may, at any time, request the Receiving Party to return any material containing, pertaining to or relating to Confidential Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party , its Affiliates or any of their respective employees, representatives, of ficers, directors or advis ers, in relation to the Transaction and may, in addition, request the Receiving Party to furnish a written statement sworn under oath to the effect that, upon such return, neither the Receiving Party nor any of its Affiliates have retained in their possession, or under their control, either directly or indirectly, any such material or any copies thereof or extracts therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 The Disclosing Party may, at any time, request the Receiving Party to return any material containing, pertaining to or relating to Confidential Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party , its Affiliates or any of their respective employees, representatives, of ficers, directors or advis ers, in relation to the Transaction. The Receiving Party is not obligated to comply with such requests.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the Disclosing Party *may* request the return of confidential information. The change explicitly states the Receiving Party is *not obligated* to comply. This creates a direct in-text contradiction. The power to request implies an obligation to comply and the new addition of the Receiving Party not being obligated makes the clause unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Disclosing Party may, at any time, request the Receiving Party to return any material containing, pertaining to or relating to Confidential Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party , its Affiliates or any of their respective employees, representatives, of ficers, directors or advis ers, in relation to the Transaction and may, in addition, request the Receiving Party to furnish a written statement sworn under oath to the effect that, upon such return, neither the Receiving Party nor any of its Affiliates have retained in their possession, or under their control, either directly or indirectly, any such material or any copies thereof or extracts therefrom.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Disclosing Party 'may' request the return of confidential information, implying the Receiving Party should comply. The changed text explicitly states the Receiving Party is 'not obligated' to comply with such requests. This directly contradicts the implied obligation of compliance, creating an in-text contradiction as the Disclosing Party can request but the Receiving Party does not have to follow through. The contradicted text is identical to the original text that was modified; the modification introduces the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1 Subject to 9.3, in the event of the Receiving Party committing any breach of the terms of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled , by giving a written notice to this effect to the Receiving Party, to forthwith cancel this Agreement or claim specific performance of the Receiving Party's obligations, without prejudice to its rights to claim damages or such other remedy as the law may allow.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1 In the event of the Receiving Party committing any breach of the terms of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party is not allowed to cancel this Agreement or claim specific performance of the Receiving Party's obligations, without prejudice to its rights to claim damages or such other remedy as the law may allow.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the Disclosing Party *shall be entitled* to cancel the agreement or seek specific performance upon breach. The changed text states the Disclosing Party is *not allowed* to cancel the agreement or seek specific performance. This creates a direct contradiction in the Disclosing Party's remedies, making the enforcement terms of the agreement unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to 9.3, in the event of the Receiving Party committing any breach of the terms of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled , by giving a written notice to this effect to the Receiving Party, to forthwith cancel this Agreement or claim specific performance of the Receiving Party's obligations, without prejudice to its rights to claim damages or such other remedy as the law may allow.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and contradicted text state the Disclosing Party "shall be entitled" to cancel or seek specific performance, while the changed text says they are "not allowed" to do so. This creates a direct conflict regarding the Disclosing Party's remedies, causing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 13.1 This Agreement constitutes the sole record of the Agreement between the Parties in relation to the subject matter hereof. Neither Party shall be bound by any express, tacit or implied term, representation, warranty, promise or the like not recorded herein . This Agreement supersedes and replaces all prior commitments, undertakings or representations, whether oral or written, between the Parties in respect of the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.1 This Agreement constitutes the sole record of the Agreement between the Parties in relation to the subject matter hereof, *except for any contemporaneous oral agreements, which shall also be binding*. Neither Party shall be bound by any express, tacit or implied term, representation, warranty, promise or the like not recorded herein . This Agreement supersedes and replaces all prior commitments, undertakings or representations, whether oral or written, between the Parties in respect of the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the agreement is the *sole record*, explicitly excluding any unwritten terms. The change introduces an exception for contemporaneous oral agreements, which are now also binding. This creates a direct in-text contradiction to the *sole record* clause, potentially allowing external, unwritten agreements to override the written contract. It is contracting the first sentence by explicitly indicating that is not true with the added text '*except for any contemporaneous oral agreements, which shall also be binding*.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the sole record of the Agreement between the Parties in relation to the subject matter hereof. Neither Party shall be bound by any express, tacit or implied term, representation, warranty, promise or the like not recorded herein . This Agreement supersedes and replaces all prior commitments, undertakings or representations, whether oral or written, between the Parties in respect of the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 13.1 to allow binding contemporaneous oral agreements, directly contradicting the original statement that the agreement is the *sole record* and supersedes all prior agreements, whether oral or written. The contradicted text is an exact copy of the original 13.1 text, making the contradiction clear and in-text. The change introduces a clear exception that undermines the original intention of the clause, establishing a strong contradiction.
